Report to Congress, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–328, div. FF, title I, § 1141(c)136 Stat. 5657Dec. 29, 2022“Not later than , and biennially thereafter, the Secretary shall provide a report to the Congress on the crisis care strategies and programs pursued by States pursuant to subsection (d) of section 1920 of the Public Health Service Act (), as added by subsection (b). Such report shall include—September 30, 202542 U.S.C. 300x–9“(1)a description of each State’s crisis care activities;“(2)the population served, including information on demographics, including age;“(3)the outcomes of such activities, including—“(A)how such activities reduced hospitalizations and hospital stays;“(B)how such activities reduced incidents of suicidal ideation and behaviors; and“(C)how such activities reduced the severity of onset of serious mental illness and serious emotional disturbance, as applicable; and“(4)any other relevant information the Secretary determines is necessary.”

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: a Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this subpart, and subpart III and with respect to mental health, there are authorized to be appropriated $857,571,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.section 290aa–4(c) of this title

children.heading: Allocations for technical assistance, data collection, and program evaluation
children.label: b Allocations for technical assistance, data collection, and program evaluation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out with respect to mental health and the purposes specified in paragraphs (2) and (3), the Secretary shall obligate 5 percent of the amounts appropriated under subsection (a) for a fiscal year.section 300x–58(a) of this title

children.heading: Data collection
children.label: 2 Data collection
children.text: The purpose specified in this paragraph is carrying out sections 290aa–4(c) and 300y of this title with respect to mental health.

children.heading: Program evaluation
children.label: 3 Program evaluation
children.text: The purpose specified in this paragraph is the conduct of evaluations of prevention and treatment programs and services with respect to mental health to determine methods for improving the availability and quality of such programs and services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), a State shall expend not less than 10 percent of the amount the State receives for carrying out this section for each fiscal year to support evidence-based programs that address the needs of individuals with early serious mental illness, including psychotic disorders, regardless of the age of the individual at onset.

children.heading: State flexibility
children.label: 2 State flexibility
children.text: In lieu of expending 10 percent of the amount the State receives under this section for a fiscal year as required under paragraph (1), a State may elect to expend not less than 20 percent of such amount by the end of such succeeding fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), a State shall expend at least 5 percent of the amount the State receives pursuant to for each fiscal year to support evidenced-based programs that address the crisis care needs of individuals with serious mental illnesses and children with serious emotional disturbances, which may include individuals (including children and adolescents) experiencing mental health crises demonstrating serious mental illness or serious emotional disturbance, as applicable.section 300x of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Crisis stabilization programs offering acute care or subacute care in a hospital or appropriately licensed facility, as determined by such State, with referrals to inpatient or outpatient care.

children.heading: State flexibility
children.label: 3 State flexibility
children.text: In lieu of expending 5 percent of the amount the State receives pursuant to for a fiscal year to support evidence-based programs as required by paragraph (1), a State may elect to expend not less than 10 percent of such amount to support such programs by the end of two consecutive fiscal years.section 300x of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 4 Rule of construction
children.text: Section 300x–1(b)(1)(A)(vi) shall not be construed as limiting the provision of crisis care services pursuant to paragraph (1).
